old_claim.note: Heavy Draft Plowing provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Yokes & Harnesses provides traction arrangements for animal-powered treading or threshing sledges; heavy draft plowing is a related field use rather than a direct prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: The overview describes threshing boards or sledges pulled by oxen, mules, or horses over grain.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Animal-powered threshing depends on animal traction arrangements, not specifically on heavy draft plowing.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected edge points to the harnessing/traction technology used to pull threshing sledges and supports animal-driven grain separation without requiring a separate plowing technology.

ontology_before: Animal-Powered Threshing depended directly on heavy_draft_plowing, conflating threshing with plow traction.
ontology_after: Animal-Powered Threshing depends on threshing_and_winnowing, animal_husbandry, and yokes_and_harnesses as the direct traction predecessor.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: heavy_draft_plowing is no longer direct, and yokes_and_harnesses is now a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to plow-based field preparation rather than threshing.
A stronger source showed early animal-powered threshing required heavy draft plows.
Yokes and harnesses were split so that threshing-sledge traction had a more specific direct node.
short_reason: Animal-powered threshing needs animal traction, not heavy draft plowing specifically.
